title: parablechnum decorum
parts: Parablechnum decorum has many medicinal parts, such as its leaves, roots, and stems, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The leaves of Parablechnum decorum are rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, including quercetin and kaempferol, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The roots of the plant contain saponins and triterpenoids, such as oleanolic acid, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The stems of Parablechnum decorum are also rich in alkaloids, including berberine, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-diabetic properties.
